name: Multi Device
use: Bidirectional or iterative multi-device composition: sample between devices, arrange, layer, and build songs across hardware
overview: Framework for chaining hardware devices via audio routing. Two modes: (1) Bidirectional — create on Device A, sample to Device B, arrange, resample back to A for multi-layer compositions; (2) Iterative — create loops on Device A, sample to Device B, do all arrangement on B (no roundtrip). Enables fast iteration without manual re-recording. Works with any two devices supporting audio in/out and sampling/sequencing. Requires audio cables and BPM sync.
workflow: Setup (one-time): Connect Woovebox audio out → P-6 line in (for forward sampling). Connect P-6 audio out → Woovebox line in (for reverse sampling). Set both to same BPM: Woovebox — Song mode (hold [Value] + short-press [16/A8]) → GLob page → hold [1/Cd] + turn [Value] to set BPM. P-6 — turn [TEMPO/VALUE] to set BPM. ★

Forward: Woovebox → P-6 sample. (1) On Woovebox: create loop via Song mode → enable tracks → press [Play] to start. (2) On P-6: press [SAMPLING] → select blinking pad (destination 1–6) → press [SAMPLING] to start → press [SAMPLING] to stop. Loop now in pad, ready to sequence.

Arrange on P-6: Set pattern length — [SHIFT] + [D' LAST] → turn [TEMPO/VALUE] → [EXIT]. Step input: press pad → press step buttons 1–16 to place hits (lit = active). Duplicate pattern: [SHIFT] + [COPY] → [VALUE] → 'DUPL' → [ENTER]. Queue next: hold [PATTERN] + press step (switches at end). Optional: route to effects via [MFX] + pad to toggle BUS A ↔ BUS EFFECT.

Reverse: P-6 → Woovebox sample. (1) On P-6: press [COPY ►] to start pattern playback. (2) On Woovebox: enter Sampler (hold [Value] + short-press [14/A6]) → hold [Play] + short-press 1–16 to select kit → turn [Value] to choose input format (44.8 line for loops) → press [Play] to monitor VU → hold [Write] + hold slot 1–16 to record during P-6 playback → switch to any track to exit and confirm.

Use re-sampled loop in Woovebox: Apply kit to track — context menu (hold [Write] + short-press [Value]) → 'SMPK Pach' → switch to track → 'Pste Pach'. Slices available on SEq page. Program steps: hold [Write] + short-press step buttons 1–16. Optional: auto-chop for melodic re-sequencing.

Nested loop workflow: Create initial loop on Woovebox (bass + kick) → sample to P-6 pad 1 → arrange 8-step pattern → sample back to Woovebox track → layer with original tracks. Iterate: add layer on P-6, sample back, add track to Woovebox. Each roundtrip adds depth.
Setup: Connect Woovebox audio out → SmplTrek line in. Set both to same BPM: Woovebox — Song mode → GLob page → hold [1/Cd] + turn [Value]. SmplTrek — System menu / scene settings. ★

Create loop on Woovebox: Sketch melodic/chord loop via Song mode — enable tracks (Cd, bS, Ld, etc.) → set fragment length → hold [Write] + short-press tracks to enable → press [Play]. Build 8–16 bar loop.
Record to SmplTrek: Start Woovebox playback. On SmplTrek: hold a track pad (Loop Track) → select input source → confirm → record duration of loop. Repeat for additional melodic passes.

Arrange on SmplTrek: Hold Drum Track pad → select drum kit → program drums in step sequencer. Add sidechaining: [Mixer] → select track → turn knobs for level, pan, send FX. Layer extra melodies: record new Woovebox loops, sample each to separate tracks.

Iterative workflow: Create new Woovebox loop (verse/chorus/bridge) → Woovebox playback → SmplTrek: hold track pad → quick sample → arrange drums to match → edit effects. Each iteration adds a section; no roundtrip. Export: [Mixer] → Mixdown → [OK].

Final composition in SmplTrek: Use scenes for song structure (Intro → Verse → Chorus → Bridge → Outro). Press [PLAY] for full project (auto-advance scenes) or [STEP] buttons to launch individual scenes live.
